title: MemoryCapsule persistence via KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1
goal: Persist MemoryCapsule audit records through the existing KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 mutation path (per packet forbidden_paths: 'No mutations through the backend inspector plane') so capsule history is durable, replayable, and review-gated. The persisted shape is the CapsuleAuditLog plus capsule metadata (not the full MemoryPack item contents, which already live in FEMS).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/persistence.rs (CapsuleRecorder + CapsuleRecord typed shape) defining a KernelActionCatalogV1 action variant `CapsuleAuditRecord`. Wiring uses the existing kernel/write_box surface; no new mutation path. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/memory_persistence_tests.rs using a mock KernelActionCatalogV1.
unblock_conditions: MT-141/142/143 ship the capsule primitives + builder. KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1 already exist in main from KERNEL-002.
implementation_notes: Per the WP-KERNEL-004 packet forbidden_paths the ONLY mutation paths are KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1; no parallel mutation. CapsuleRecorder must therefore submit a typed action via the existing catalog, not invent a new write path. Surface: `pub struct CapsuleRecorder<'a> { action_catalog: &'a dyn KernelActionSubmitter }` with `pub fn record(record: CapsuleRecord) -> Result<RecordReceipt, RecorderError>`. CapsuleRecord shape: `{ capsule_id: Uuid (v7), capsule_source_hash: String, task_type: TaskType, policy: RetrievalPolicy, audit_log: CapsuleAuditLog, built_at_utc, recorded_at_utc, session_id, role_id, outcome: Option<CapsuleOutcome> }` where CapsuleOutcome is filled later by the outcome feedback loop (MT-158). RecordReceipt: `{ record_id: Uuid v7, write_box_envelope_id: Uuid v7, persisted_at_utc }`. Use serde_json for envelope payload; the catalog routes to durable storage via the existing WriteBoxV1 review path (review-gated per FEMS V1 invariant). Tests: recorder constructs a well-formed catalog action; record receipt round-trips; recorder fails cleanly on KernelActionCatalogV1 rejection with typed RecorderError; outcome field defaults to None at initial record.
risk_if_missed: Without persistence via the canonical mutation path, capsule audit history fragments across ad-hoc stores and the no-parallel-mutation invariant is violated.
validator_focus: All writes flow through KernelActionCatalogV1; no direct database calls. CapsuleRecord captures the full audit log so replay is possible without re-running retrieval.

lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator review found the implementation pattern (surface-shape + trait abstraction + LiveXxxUnavailable / placeholder props) satisfied test contracts but did not satisfy what the Master Spec actually says these things do. None of the 27 MTs listed below are correct implementations. All 27 are reopened and must be redone before WP-KERNEL-004 can be considered complete. No other MT may be claimed until these 27 are correctly implemented and re-validated. MT-148 work was rolled back; it is to be revisited after these 27 close correctly.
lifecycle.deflection_summary: Same as MT-141. MemoryCapsule persistence via KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1: the catalog action shape committed without verifying it matches the catalog's actual register/dispatch contract for this MT's purpose.
lifecycle.wp_hard_fail_note: WP-KERNEL-004 cannot complete with these 27 MTs in NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION status. Each MT below is individually hard-failed AND counts as a hard-fail blocker on the WP itself. The committed code from the prior session remains on the feat branch as a base to either deepen or replace; the lifecycle status below is the source of truth for whether the MT is done, not the presence of source files.

autonomous_session_claim.note: Autonomous KERNEL_BUILDER session triage following the catastrophic-build-broken finding: this MT cannot be honestly implemented in the current product-code state because model_runtime cannot compile. Implementation deferred to a future session running against a restored build.

adversarial_review.adversarial_defect: Adversarial caught Sub-rule 2 violation: CapsuleRecorder accepts `&dyn KernelActionSubmitter` trait, and the proof test uses `CapturingSubmitter` (an in-memory `RefCell<Vec<KernelActionSubmission>>` authored in the same diff). No real Postgres row is written; the test asserts only schema-correct action formation. The brief explicitly states 'Real Postgres at POSTGRES_TEST_URL counts' as the satisfying resource touch. REWORK REQUIRED: add an integration test that binds the recorder to a real KernelActionCatalogV1 backed by real Postgres at POSTGRES_TEST_URL, then exercises recorder.record(sample_capsule) and asserts the kernel_action_catalog Postgres table received the row.
adversarial_review.triggers_brief_rework_loop: Per brief Phase 2 step 'If verdict is FAIL_NEEDS_REWORK: fix the defects, rerun proof, spawn a fresh adversarial sub-agent.' Implementation must change before next adversarial round.

residual_risks: Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator verdict remains PENDING.
Focused Postgres/live-kernel commands were run, not a full workspace cargo test.

minimum_controls: All persistence flows through KernelActionCatalogV1 + WriteBoxV1; no parallel mutation path.
CapsuleRecord captures the full audit log so replay does not depend on re-running retrieval.
RecorderError is typed so call sites cannot silently swallow persistence failures.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: CapsuleRecorder builds KernelActionRequestV1 + WriteBoxV1Envelope using the live kernel action_envelope + write_boxes primitives, with deterministic payload_sha256 and proper schema_id constants — matches spec 6.5 mechanical-integration shape.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: CapsuleRecorder builds KernelActionRequestV1 + WriteBoxV1Envelope using the live kernel action_envelope + write_boxes primitives, with deterministic payload_sha256 and proper schema_id constants — matches spec 6.5 mechanical-integration shape.
